rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ANGELA MOSS SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #9, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/28/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Are the state departments not entitled to notice? Is the beneficiary not receiving any public benefits? (The 5/14/07 order establishing this SNT required satisfaction of all liens in favor of the three state agencies first, upon the death of the beneficiary.) For what costs was the attorney paid $460 on 11/10/20? There are no liabilities, but it appears that the trustee did not pay herself, as conservator, the full amount of fees she was authorized to on 4/16/21. The petition does not address the amount of bond or whether it is sufficient. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 2/17/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MARION HALL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Relief From Breach Of Conservatoship, To Remove Conservator And Appoint Successor Conservator And Objection To Post-Move Notice Of Chnage Of Residence Of Conservatee
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. No proposed order has been submitted yet. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] Clearly, conservatorships are JC forms beginnning with GC. Examiner erred in referring to forms in decedents' estates (beginning with "DE".) A person seeking appointment as a conservator must file the form GC-310, etc. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. PLEASE HAVE YOUR CASE NUMBER AND NAME READY FOR THE CLERK.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARIA E. CASTILLO 2020 TRUST DTD JULY 24, 2020; and THE MARIA E. CASTILLO SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST,
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for counsels to report the court with the status of the case. Also, Petitioner (Ms. Lopez)'s counsel to address the following issues: 1)appraisal date appears inconsistent. If the parties disagree on valuation, a party may need to seek appointment of a probate referee and file I&A; 2) copies of escrow statement to be provided. Two parcels of r/p reported as sold (Justin Drive and Chattanooga St.); 3) Clarification if there have been any rental income prior to sale, especially from Chattanooga Street property which is a 6-unit property and not sold until Nov. of 2021; 4) bank statements showing ending balances; 5) Clarification of (a) the total funds that Ms. Lopez has advanced to the Trust and (b) the total amount that the Trust is liable for as of the account end period. Schedule A ($47,342.86) refers to the Liabilities schedule, and Schedule C shows some expenses have been reimbursed ($1,450 and $2,000). If all balance amounts (in bold) listed on "LIABILITIES" schedule are the trust's current liabilities to petitioner. Ms. Brown to identify whom she represents and to address conflicting interests. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLORIA JEAN WASH RILEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/08/2023 (no earlier date available). 1) No DE-147 form is on file. 2) Item 5 of the DE-121 form must be checked. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must also be attached to and served with the DE-121 form. 3) Items 2d(1) and 3e(1) of the petition: The bond waiver provision included in Article IV of the will was amended "in its entirety" by the codicil. Article IV as amended does not waive a bond. Even if the trustee of the devisee trust waives a bond, a declaration required by LRSF 14.16(F) must be filed to state the total amount of anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate. A minimum bond ($20,000) will be required, as the proposed executor is not a resident of California. 4) Item 3f(2): Unlike the codicil, the will does not appear to be self-proving, as the witnesses did not state the place of execution required by CCP section 2015.5 & Probate Code section 8220(b). A DE-131 form must be filed. 5) The names and dates of death of the decedent's parents, siblings, and their issue must be provided. 6) No courtesy copy of the petition has been received. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE FLORENCE C. HAIMES 2001 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee Due To Unavailability, And On Waiver Of Bond
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/7/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: If all distributions have been made to the beneficiaries of the pecuniary bequests, please file the receipts or other proof of their interests having been satisfied. (Why was notice given to them if their interests were satisfied?) An appearance may be required for the Court to determine if the hourly rates for the proposed successor trustee and her attorney are appropriate for the Court to authorize; this appears to be something that should be arranged between the attorney and the beneficiary/beneficiaries. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 2/24/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: WAYNE H. ALBA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION Petition For Order Confirming Ownership Of Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/08/2023 (no earlier date available) for the following issues to be addressed (e-mailed to counsel on 01/20/2023): 1) There is an existing case opened in May 2022 for the subject estate. The case number is PES-22-305321. A drop-off ex parte petition must be filed to consolidate the two cases. PES-22-305321 must be the lead case. All subsequent papers must be filed in the lead case. 2) Please provide a copy of Mark Alba's Schedule E attached to his tax return. 3) Please provide a proof of each petitioner's payment or other financial contribution toward the purchase price of the subject real property. 4) Please provide a copy of the closing escrow statement or purchase contract for the subject real property if available. 5) Was each interest claimed to belong to each petitioner acquired with community property, separate property, or both? Please provide facts in support of the property characterization. 6) Is each petitioner, other than Katherine Alba-Swanson, requesting confirmation of his/her interest as his/her separate property? 7) Should each petitioner's spouse including Katherine Alba-Swanson's former spouse be served with this petition in the manner required by Probate Code section 851(a)(2)? 8) Does Brendan Alba have sufficient legal capacity without requiring a guardian ad litem? Is a separate special needs trust already in existence for his benefit? Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
